title: Mathematics
subtitle: HKDSE Compulsory Part (S4–S6)
intro: Topic units across three strands. The map shows required prerequisites; the list follows a readable teaching order.

chapters.title: Counting and numbers
chapters.description: Count from zero, spot patterns, add, multiply, and divide; then meet place value and big numbers from hundreds to billions.

chapters.title: Directed numbers, rates & ratios
chapters.description: Operate with negative numbers and solve ratio, rate, and proportion problems in context.

chapters.title: Polynomials: expansion & factorisation
chapters.description: Expand products, collect like terms, and factorise expressions using common factors and identities.

chapters.title: Algebraic fractions & formulas
chapters.description: Simplify algebraic fractions and change the subject of formulas in applied contexts.

chapters.title: Linear equations in two unknowns
chapters.description: Solve simultaneous linear equations by substitution, elimination, and graphical interpretation.

chapters.title: Linear inequalities in one unknown
chapters.description: Solve and represent linear inequalities on the number line and in word problems.

chapters.title: Laws of indices
chapters.description: Apply index laws to simplify expressions with integer and rational exponents.

chapters.title: Quadratic equations
chapters.description: Solve quadratics by factorising, completing the square, and the formula. Model real problems with parabolas and roots.

chapters.title: Factor & remainder theorems
chapters.description: Use the factor and remainder theorems to factorise higher-degree polynomials and solve related equations.

chapters.title: Complex numbers
chapters.description: Represent complex numbers in Cartesian form and perform operations including conjugates and division.

chapters.title: Functions & graphs
chapters.description: Study domains, ranges, transformations, and graphs of common functions including quadratics and rationals.

chapters.title: Exponential & logarithmic functions
chapters.description: Explore laws of logarithms, exponential growth and decay, and sketch related graphs.

chapters.title: Sequences & series
chapters.description: Work with arithmetic and geometric sequences, sigma notation, and finite summation formulas.

chapters.title: Variations
chapters.description: Model direct, inverse, joint, and partial variation and solve combined variation problems.

chapters.title: Linear programming
chapters.description: Graph linear inequalities, identify feasible regions, and optimise objective functions in two variables.

chapters.title: Coordinate geometry foundations
chapters.description: Plot points, find distances and midpoints, and work with slopes of lines in the Cartesian plane.

chapters.title: Deductive geometry
chapters.description: Prove angle and side relationships for intersecting lines, triangles, and polygons.

chapters.title: Congruence & similarity
chapters.description: Apply congruence and similarity criteria to justify properties of triangles and scale figures.

chapters.title: Pythagoras' theorem
chapters.description: Use Pythagoras' theorem and its converse in 2D problems and coordinate settings.

chapters.title: Introduction to trigonometry
chapters.description: Define sine, cosine, and tangent ratios and solve right-angled triangle problems.

chapters.title: Mensuration in 3D
chapters.description: Find volumes and surface areas of prisms, cylinders, pyramids, cones, and spheres.

chapters.title: Coordinate geometry of circles
chapters.description: Find equations of circles, tangents, and intersections using coordinate methods and distance.

chapters.title: Advanced trigonometry
chapters.description: Use sine and cosine rules, radians, and trigonometric graphs in 2D and 3D applications.

chapters.title: Properties of circles
chapters.description: Apply chord, tangent, and angle theorems in circles to prove results and solve geometry problems.

chapters.title: Loci
chapters.description: Describe and construct loci in the plane, linking geometric constraints to algebraic equations.

chapters.title: Charts & averages
chapters.description: Organise data with tables and charts and calculate mean, median, and mode.

chapters.title: Measures of dispersion
chapters.description: Interpret range, interquartile range, variance, and standard deviation for comparing data sets.

chapters.title: Basic probability
chapters.description: Calculate theoretical and experimental probabilities for simple events and complements.

chapters.title: Permutations, combinations & probability
chapters.description: Count outcomes with permutations and combinations, then compute probabilities in finite sample spaces.

chapters.title: Normal distribution & z-scores
chapters.description: Model continuous data with the normal curve, standardise with z-scores, and read probabilities from tables.

chapters.title: Statistical literacy
chapters.description: Evaluate claims in media and research: sampling bias, correlation versus causation, and misleading charts.
